creator: Hartmann@FreeBSD.org
text:
While compiling port devel/subversion-freebsd, the port devel/apr1 gets compiled, too. In my case, this is either 
apr-ipv6-devrandom-gdbm-db48-ldap24-1.4.5.1.3.12_1
or
apr-ipv6-devrandom-gdbm-db48-postgresql90-ldap24-1.4.5.1.3.12_1.

Compiling devel/apr1 (as it is choosen by the port subversion-freebsd) via the legacy compiler gcc4.2, subversion is all right. It doesn't matter wether subversion was compiled via CLANG or legacy gcc4.2. Compiling devel/apr1 with CLANG, compilation works without error. But the subversion binary "svn" crashes and coredumps, and it doesn't matter wether subversion was compiled with CLANG or legacy gcc4.2. So I suspect some problems with the apr1-port.

How-To-Repeat: Compile devel/apr1 with CLANG and use subversion. See subversion crashing.
